note: Full grades 1-8 ladder. Ordered list IS the ladder; sequence_position assigned from index 0..N at seed time.

skills.name: Count in steps of 2, 3 and 5, and in tens from any number
Read, write and represent numbers to at least 100 in numerals and words
Recognise the place value of each digit (tens and ones)
Compare and order numbers to 100 using <, > and =
Add and subtract two-digit numbers and ones, and two-digit numbers and tens
Recall and use addition and subtraction facts to 20 fluently
Recall and use multiplication and division facts for the 2, 5 and 10 tables
Recognise and find 1/3, 1/4, 2/4 and 3/4 of a shape or quantity
Tell and write the time to five minutes, including quarter past/to the hour
Recognise and use symbols for pounds and pence and solve money problems
Identify properties of 2-D and 3-D shapes, including sides, vertices and faces
Interpret and construct pictograms, tally charts and simple tables
Read, write and order numbers up to 1000 and recognise place value
Count in multiples of 4, 8, 50 and 100
Add and subtract numbers up to three digits using formal written methods
Recall and use multiplication and division facts for the 3, 4 and 8 tables
Multiply two-digit numbers by one-digit numbers using written methods
Find and write unit and non-unit fractions of quantities and sets
Add and subtract fractions with the same denominator within one whole
Recognise, compare and find equivalent fractions with small denominators
Measure, compare, add and subtract lengths, mass and volume/capacity
Tell the time on analogue and 12-/24-hour clocks and find durations
Identify right angles and compare angles as greater or less than a right angle
Draw 2-D shapes and identify horizontal, vertical, perpendicular and parallel lines
Interpret and present data using bar charts, pictograms and tables
Recognise place value in four-digit numbers
Round numbers to the nearest 10, 100 or 1000
Count backwards through zero with negative numbers
Add and subtract four-digit numbers using written methods
Recall multiplication and division facts up to 12 × 12
Multiply two- and three-digit numbers by a one-digit number
Recognise and find equivalent fractions
Add and subtract fractions with the same denominator
Understand decimals with one and two decimal places
Convert between units of measure (e.g. kilometres to metres)
Find the perimeter and area of rectilinear shapes
Classify shapes and identify acute and obtuse angles
Plot and describe coordinates in the first quadrant
Interpret bar charts and time graphs
Read, write and order numbers to 1,000,000
Round numbers to the nearest power of 10
Multiply four-digit numbers by one- or two-digit numbers
Identify factors, multiples, primes, squares and cubes
Add and subtract fractions with related denominators
Multiply proper fractions by whole numbers
Connect percentages to fractions and decimals
Calculate area and perimeter and estimate volume
Measure and draw angles in degrees
Read and draw line graphs and tables
Use long multiplication and long division
Use the order of operations (BODMAS/BIDMAS)
Add, subtract, multiply and divide fractions
Convert between fractions, decimals and percentages
Solve problems using ratio and proportion
Use scale factors and find percentages of amounts
Use simple formulae and generate linear sequences
Find the area of triangles and parallelograms
Identify parts of a circle and plot coordinates in four quadrants
Interpret pie charts and calculate the mean
Order positive and negative integers and use the number line
Add, subtract, multiply and divide positive and negative integers
Use the order of operations (BIDMAS), factors, multiples, primes, HCF and LCM
Convert between fractions, decimals and percentages
Add, subtract, multiply and divide fractions and mixed numbers
Find percentages of amounts and solve simple percentage change problems
Simplify ratios and divide a quantity in a given ratio
Use algebraic notation and substitute values into expressions
Simplify and manipulate expressions by collecting like terms
Solve one- and two-step linear equations in one unknown
Calculate areas of rectangles, triangles and parallelograms, and perimeters
Use angle facts at a point, on a line and in triangles to find missing angles
Calculate and interpret mean, median, mode and range
Use powers, roots and standard form (index notation)
Round to significant figures and decimal places and use estimation to check
Solve percentage increase, decrease and reverse percentage problems
Solve problems using direct and inverse proportion and unitary methods
Expand single brackets and factorise expressions into a single bracket
Solve linear equations with the unknown on both sides and with brackets
Generate sequences from term-to-term and position-to-term (nth term) rules
Plot and interpret straight-line graphs and find gradient and intercept
Calculate the area and circumference of circles
Use angles in parallel lines and properties of quadrilaterals and polygons
Perform and describe reflections, rotations, translations and enlargements
Calculate probabilities of single events and use the probability scale
Construct and interpret pie charts, scatter graphs and grouped data
Calculate with standard form and apply the laws of indices
Solve repeated percentage change and compound interest problems
Use ratio and scale factors with similar shapes and proportional reasoning
Expand and factorise quadratic expressions
Rearrange formulae to change the subject
Solve simultaneous linear equations algebraically and graphically
Find the equation of a straight line in the form y = mx + c
Apply Pythagoras' theorem to find missing sides in right-angled triangles
Calculate the volume and surface area of prisms and cylinders
Use trigonometric ratios (sine, cosine and tangent) in right-angled triangles
Use tree diagrams and sample space to find probabilities of combined events
Interpret scatter graphs, correlation and lines of best fit
